title: Preliminary Estimated Flu Disease Burden 2024-2025 Flu Season | Flu Burden | CDC
text:
number of flu illnesses, medical visits, hospitalizations, and deaths. This is a way to understand the impact of the flu season on the population and the healthcare system. The icons are used to represent the different outcomes, and the estimates are provided in a range from the lowest possible number to the highest possible number. The estimates are presented in a range instead of a point value since flu circulation is ongoing during this period and some of the data that contribute to the model may be limited, especially early in the season. These ranges are updated each week during the flu season to reflect the most up to date information.
Show Text Description 
Each week CDC calculates a lower estimate and an upper estimate of flu-related hospitalizations that have occurred since the beginning of the season (October 1, 2024). These are compared to end-of-season estimates of flu-related hospitalizations from previous flu seasons. These calculations allow for a preliminary comparison of how in-season flu-related hospitalizations are tracking in relation to past seasons. This can help health officials to continue to manage impacts on health care systems and promote prevention measures.
Text Description 
This graphic visually displays the estimated disease burden of flu during the 2024-2025 flu season by describing the number of cumulative flu-related hospitalizations. This is
